title: Xanthostemon chrysanthus
taxonomy: Xanthostemon chrysanthus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is commonly known as the yellow candlewood or golden candlewood. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia, specifically in the northern parts of Queensland. Xanthostemon chrysanthus is classified as a shrub or small tree, typically growing up to 6-10 meters tall. It is known for its bright yellow flowers and is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens.
uses: Xanthostemon chrysanthus, commonly known as the Australian Christmas bush, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
